methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Biobot Analytics is a Series B biotech company founded by Mariana Matus and Newsha Ghaeli that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private-stage peers and 3 public biotech companies operating in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. The Stage-Progression method, using the $40M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $76M. The Peer-Median method anchors to five same-sector, same-stage comparables: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M). The median of these five peers is $810M, with a range of $600M–$3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.6x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) was not applicable due to the absence of revenue data.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $65M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $443M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the peer-median method is skewed upward by outliers like Eikon and Neko Health, while stage-progression relies on raised capital as a proxy in the absence of revenue or profitability metrics. Key uncertainties include Biobot's revenue trajectory, competitive positioning relative to the peer set, and whether the outlier valuations represent comparable business models or stage risk.
